items.setting: Listen to an announcement at a school art exhibit.
questions: What is the purpose of the announcement?
What will the speaker probably do next?
items.transcript: Man: Thank you for touring this special collection of paintings we recently acquired from a donor. We're going to begin with late 18th century works in the hallway on your left. We'll then proceed to early 19th century in the next room and finish with the modern art collection on the next floor. But first, I'll pass out a detailed map of the collection, which includes some artist information on the back.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a university classroom.
questions: What field does the guest speaker most likely work in?
What does the speaker imply about the guest lecturer's talk?
items.transcript: Man: I'm pleased to announce that we'll have a guest speaker next week as part of our unit on environmental policy. Dr. Maya Tan will join us on Thursday and she'll be speaking about international climate agreements, the role of youth activism, and current challenges in global sustainability efforts. There will be time for questions at the end, so feel free to prepare some in advance. This talk should give you valuable insights for your upcoming group project, so please make sure to be there.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a classroom.
questions: What is available on the speaker's website?
Where should the listeners look for theories and strategies?
items.transcript: Woman: As you know, this is the final course required for your business degree. The course will operate as an independent study. You will each select an area of focus that interests you. Some examples are available on my website. Then, every week, you'll be required to submit short essays that respond to questions I give you on this topic. You'll need to refer to what you've learned in your other business courses so you can cite theories or strategies that support your responses.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a classroom for your upcoming project.
items.transcript: Please write a detailed report on a topic related to environmental science. It should be about 1500 words long and include at least two scholarly sources. Please follow the university formatting guidelines if you have any questions. My office hours are on Tuesdays and Thursdays. Just keep in mind my office hours on the last Thursday of this month will be moved forward a day to Wednesday because I'll be travelling to a research symposium.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement at a school art exhibit.
items.transcript: Thank you for touring this special collection of paintings we recently acquired from a donor. We're going to begin with late 18th century works in the hallway on your left. We'll then proceed to early 19th century in the next room and finish with the modern art collection on the next floor. But first I'll pass out a detailed map of the collection, which includes some artist information on the back.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement on a campus radio station.
items.transcript: Due to yesterday's heavy rainstorms, a major leak has developed in the roof of the library. The campus facilities department has determined that it needs to be repaired as soon as possible to avoid damage to books and library resources. To allow for maintenance work, the library will be closed until noon on Wednesday. Some library resources will still be available online, and study space will be available at the student center. The library staff apologizes for the inconvenience and appreciates your understanding.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement at a gathering of student club leaders.
items.transcript: The university is making some changes to the policies governing student organizations. One of these changes pertains to scheduling events at the last minute. This creates extra work for the Student affairs office and so, effective immediately, all student clubs must submit their event plans to the office for approval at least two weeks in advance. Failure to comply with this rule may result in the cancellation of events. If you have questions, please contact the Student affairs office.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement at a school event.
items.transcript: I have an exciting opportunity for all fashion design majors. We'll be working with an up-and-coming business downtown that focuses solely on young adult clothing. If you're in your second year or higher, you can apply for an internship with them with the possibility of being hired once you graduate. They're also accepting design submissions for their company logo, and the creator of the selected design will be paid for it. Their website has all the details.I'll give you that URL now.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement at a school art exhibit.
items.transcript: Thank you for touring this special collection of paintings we recently acquired from a donor. We're going to begin with late 18th century works in the hallway on your left. We'll then proceed to early 19th century in the next room and finish with the modern art collection on the next floor. But first, I'll pass out a detailed map of the collection, which includes some artist information on the back.

items.setting: Listen to a campus radio announcement.
items.transcript: Students, the deadline for the annual University Photography contest is quickly approaching. Remember that the theme for this year's contest is Urban Landscapes to commemorate the 50th anniversary of our city's first skyscraper. Make sure to check the guidelines on the university website for details on how to submit a photo. They have changed significantly since last year.

items.setting: Listen to a campus radio announcement.
items.transcript: Attention students. The Community Service Club will be moving to biweekly meetings instead of monthly meetings due to strong enrollment. You may still choose to attend one meeting a month, or you can come more often. It's up to you. We have some new ways for students to get involved this year, including helping at a local park, so please check your email for the full list of upcoming events. We'll be sending the message out shortly.

Attention, Club members. We wanted to make you aware of the new attendance policy that will be implemented starting next month. Due to ongoing issues with excessive absences, we will now require all members to attend at least 75% of the meetings each term to maintain their membership. Failure to comply may result in dismissal from the club. Allowances will be made for extenuating circumstances and members can appeal a dismissal. Please visit our website to view the full policy.

Attention. The fitness center will be closing in two minutes. Please wrap up your workouts. Remember to return all dumbbells, bars and other items to their proper places. As a reminder, we'll be closed for our cleanup day tomorrow, but we hope to see you again when we reopen on Saturday morning the day after tomorrow. Have a good night and a safe trip home.

Good morning, senior class. Starting next semester, all graduating students must complete a professional development workshop before receiving their diplomas. This requirement ensures you are equipped with essential career skills, including resume writing, interview preparation, and networking strategies. Sessions will be available throughout the semester, but they may fill up fast, so plan ahead and sign up early. Check the university website for details on scheduling and registration. Your future success starts with preparation.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement at a university gym.
questions: What can be inferred about the gym schedule?
What gym rule does the speaker emphasize?
items.transcript: Attention, students. Starting next week, the gym will be open from 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM daily. Please ensure that you wear appropriate athletic attire and footwear while using the equipment. This helps us maintain a safe and hygienic environment and prevents damage to the machinery.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement at a school event.
questions: What is the purpose of the announcement?
What can be inferred about how administrative forms were submitted previously?
items.transcript: Good afternoon students. I'd like to outline some new procedures for campus administrative activities. Beginning Monday, all administrative forms must be submitted electronically through the University's online portal. This change aims to streamline processes and reduce paper waste. Additionally, the Administrative Office will extend its hours to better accommodate student needs, opening at 9:00 AM and closing at 6:00 PM. These updates are meant to improve efficiency and accessibility for everyone. If you have any questions, please contact the Administrative Office by phone or e-mail.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a university classroom.
questions: What should be the focus of the project the speaker mentions?
What requirement of an assignment does the speaker stress?
items.transcript: Good afternoon students, Just a quick reminder about the upcoming deadline for your digital storytelling project. Final submissions are due next Friday. Your project should include a narrated video that combines images, voice over, and background music to tell a personal story. Please use the common file format. And it's very important that all media used is either original or properly credited to the source. If you have any questions about the technical requirements or need help with editing tools, feel free to reach out.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement on a university radio station.
items.transcript: Attention, students. Due to construction, we've had to temporarily reduce the number of available parking spaces on campus. Because of this, the off campus shuttle will now begin running at 6:00 AM rather than 8:00 AM. If you are driving, please pay close attention to the postage signage and make sure that you are parking in your designated area. Student cars parked in faculty lots at any time will be ticketed or towed. Please visit the Parking Services website if you need to pay a ticket.

items.setting: Listen to a campus radio announcement.
items.transcript: The due date for submitting photographs to the Campus Photography Contest is today at 3:00 PM. If you would like to enter but have not yet submitted a photograph, remember to do so today. After all submissions are in, selected photography students will judge the photos and declare a winner. The winner's name and winning photo will be printed in the campus newspaper, and all photographs entered will be posted on the university website next Wednesday.

items.setting: listen to an announcement at a University Club meeting.
items.transcript: I see we have a lot of new members here this evening. Tonight we'll focus on getting to know each other, and starting next week we'll be talking about some exciting new projects, including volunteering at local elementary schools, fundraising for local charities, and planting trees in City Park. I hope you're as excited about giving back to the broader community around our university as I am. I'll start by telling you a bit about myself and then we'll go around the room.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement by a university administration.
items.transcript: We apologize for the cold temperatures in campus buildings today. Overnight, a large steam pipe failed. The entire steam distribution system must be temporarily shut down to allow for repairs to the pipe. Unfortunately, this means that most buildings are currently without heat and hot water. Science Lab buildings have their own backup systems and should be unaffected. Please remember that the use of electric heaters is prohibited. These can overload buildings, electrical systems, and cause a power outage. We'll update everyone when Steam service is restored.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a university classroom.
items.transcript: Since a lot of you have expressed an interest in a career in education once you've graduated, I thought I'd share something that I think will be very helpful to you. It's a list of professional organizations and networking opportunities for aspiring new teachers. I've attached a copy with an e-mail I sent everyone just before class. If you're one of the students I'm referring to, I highly recommend you check it out.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a classroom.
items.transcript: I have some updates regarding the schedule for your upcoming assignments. The deadline for your research paper has been extended to next Friday, giving you an extra week to incorporate my feedback on your outline. Additionally, the date for your group presentations has been moved to the following Monday to allow you more time to practice. These changes are intended to help reduce stress during a busy part of the semester. If you have questions, visit me during office hours or send me an e-mail.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a classroom.
items.transcript: Good morning students. I have an important update regarding our classroom facilities. Starting next Monday, we will be using the University's new digital attendance system. You must scan your student ID card at the lecture hall entrance to record your presence. This system will help streamline and accurately track attendance records. Please be sure to bring your student ID to every lecture. If you encounter any issues with the scanner, notify me immediately so we can address it.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement on a university radio station.
items.transcript: Are you someone who loves writing research papers? Do you find yourself often reading something and thinking, this could use some work? Become a peer Writing Mentor. An information session will be held next Tuesday at 7pm in the campus centre. Year after year, graduating students say the Writing mentor program was the biggest factor in their academic success. This is a great opportunity to meet new people, make a positive impact and gain valuable experience. See you on Tuesday.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement on a university radio station.
items.transcript: Good morning students. I want to remind you about the new computer lab we're opening next week in Scott Hall. The lab will have advanced graphic design software and high performance computers. This was a significant financial investment, so please use the lab safely and respectfully. Also be advised that the lab is replacing the multi purpose room in Scott Hall so it will no longer be available for club meetings and such. Meeting rooms in the Student Center will still be available.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a classroom.
questions: Why has a new campus policy been put into place?
Why should students contact a campus office?
items.transcript: Attention, students. I want to explain the new campus recycling policy that will begin next Monday. Recycling bins for paper, plastic and metal will be placed in all academic buildings and residence halls. Items must be sorted correctly to avoid contamination. This policy is part of our effort to reduce waste and support environmental sustainability. We hope all of you will do your best to support a more sustainable campus. If you have questions, please contact the campus sustainability office.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement at a school event.
questions: What is the purpose of the announcement?
What can be inferred about how administrative forms were submitted previously?
items.transcript: Good afternoon, students. I'd like to outline some new procedures for campus administrative activities. Beginning Monday, all administrative forms must be submitted electronically through the University's online portal. This change aims to streamline processes and reduce paper waste. Additionally, the Administrative Office will extend its hours to better accommodate student needs. Opening at 9am and closing at 6pm These updates are meant to improve efficiency and accessibility for everyone. If you have any questions, please contact the Administrative Office by phone or email.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a university classroom.
questions: What should be the focus of the project the speaker mentions?
What requirement of an assignment does the speaker stress?
items.transcript: Good afternoon, students. Just a quick reminder about the upcoming deadline for your digital storytelling project. Final submissions are due next Friday. Your project should include a narrated video that combines images, voiceover and background music to tell a personal story. Please use a common file format and it's very important that all media used is either original or properly credited to the source. If you have any questions about the technical requirements or need help with editing tools, feel free to reach out.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a classroom.
items.transcript: Before we get started today, I want to talk about the new recycling, regulations going into effect on Monday. All recyclable materials including paper Plastics and glass must be placed in the designated blue bins located throughout the campus non-recyclable waste should go in the black bins. These measures are part of our university-wide effort to promote sustainability. If you are unsure about whether your waist is recyclable, please refer to the informational posters around campus, you'll see them everywhere.

items.setting: Listen to a school radio announcement.
items.transcript: We wanted to update everyone on the recreation facilities, schedule, starting next week. The gym and fitness center will close two hours early every night for maintenance. Both facilities will return to their usual hours. Before the end of the semester, we will inform everyone. When the work is complete, when the facility reopens everyone, using the courts in the gym will be required to change their shoes. Before entering the gym area, we suggest keeping an extra pair of shoes in your locker.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement at a school event.
items.transcript: Good evening students. We want to provide you with information regarding the upcoming community service award ceremony. The event will take place next Friday at 6 p.m. in the University Auditorium and will recognize students who have spent time giving back to the community this year, we will be introducing two new categories to recognize outstanding environmental service and outstanding service in public education. We encourage all students to attend and support their peers Community service is an important part of the university's experience.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a classroom.
items.transcript: I want to remind you all that we will have a paper due every Friday. The paper should be a response to the week's reading and should be at least 2 pages long. Papers should be uploaded to the class portal on Fridays by 5 PMI will grade them and send you feedback by the end of the weekend. Late papers will be marked down by one letter grade each day unless you speak with me ahead of time and receive an extension.

items.setting: Listen to a school radio announcement.
items.transcript: Attention students, We apologize for the inconvenience caused by the recent changes to our work study program application process. Unfortunately, due to system issues, some applications were not processed properly. We are working to resolve this problem as quickly as possible. Please resubmit your applications through the updated portal and phone the Career Center if you encounter any further issues. Additionally, we're pleased to announce several new part time work opportunities available on campus. Feel free to stop by the Center to learn more.

items.setting: Listen to an announcement in a classroom.
items.transcript: Good afternoon, students. I'm sorry for the confusion regarding the deadlines for your upcoming assignments. As I wrote in this morning's e-mail, the due dates on your syllabus are incorrect. Your research paper is actually due on Wednesday the 31st, and your group presentations are due the following Friday. I regret any inconvenience this has caused. On the other hand, this means you have some extra time to complete your assignments. If you have any questions, feel free to reach out during office hours.
